name: Chipati (Indian Griddle Fried Flat Bread)
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift the whole wheat flour and salt together in a large bowl.
2. Make a well in the center of the mixture and add the ghee and water.
3. Mix until combined, then knead for 10 minutes to form a pliable dough.
4. Form the dough into a ball, cover with a clean cloth, and let it rest for at least 1 hour, or overnight.
5. Divide the dough into 20-24 portions.
6. Roll each portion into 6-inch circles and let them rest for 5 minutes.
7. Heat a griddle or cast iron frying pan over medium-high heat until very hot.
8. Place a rolled-out chipati on the heated surface and fry for 1 minute on one side.
9. Flip the chipati and fry for another 1 minute, until golden brown and crispy.
10. Repeat with remaining portions.
Yield: 20-24 Chipati
